name: Reaper
description:
Library component. Models an external cause-of-death for a session: nondeterministically sends a Kill message at any point in the trace. Used to make crash semantics in process models more honest -- the modeled actor's death is caused by an external event (SIGTERM, OOM, parent process death, tmux pane close), not an actor-internal state transition.

This component fires Kill exactly once. Receivers handle Kill via a guarded receive triple (typically transitioning to a Crashed terminal state). TLC's exploration of when Reaper fires relative to the receiver's protocol progression is what surfaces crash-window bugs.

Used by gate-3587 specs to model 'Deacon session dies' as Reaper -> Deacon.KILL_IN, instead of Deacon making an internal nondeterministic decision to crash. See gate-3587-defense.md P1.1.

KILL_OUT.description: fires the Kill event at the bound receiver. Tagged 'Kill'. The receiver is responsible for consuming Kill in whatever state(s) crash semantics apply.

actors.name: Reaper
actors.owner: External cause-of-death sender. Pending -> Sent on send.

triples.event: external death event (SIGTERM, OOM, tmux pane close)
